name: Chili Crumb-Stuffed Onions
recipeIngredient: 4 large Texas onions (14 to 16 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare each onion for stuffing, cut a thin slice from the root end.
2. Slice off about 3/4 inch from the stem end, leaving the skin on.
3. Scoop out the onion using a small knife or apple corer and reserve for stuffing.
4. Use a spoon to make a 1/2 inch shell without cutting through the bottom of the shell.
5. Each onion will hold about 3/4 cup stuffing.
6. Finely chop enough reserved onion to measure 1 1/2 cups.
7. In a medium bowl, mix the chopped onion with the remaining ingredients except water.
8. Place onions in a baking dish.
9. Spoon filling into shells and mound up.
10. Add water to the dish and cover.
11. Bake at 400 degrees until onions are tender, about 35 to 40 minutes.
